db:abstract.db:para: Invokes a compiled nested state machine.

db:article.db:para: Invoke demonstrates how to use the <invoke> element with generated nested state-machines, where the SCXML file is compiled to a C++ class. The <invoke> element is used to create an instance of an external service.

db:section.db:title: Running the Example
db:section.db:para: To run the example from Qt Creator, open the Welcome mode and select the example from Examples. For more information, visit Building and Running an Example.

db:section.db:title: Invoking the State Machine
db:section.db:para: In statemachine.scxml, we specify a state machine with the name DirectionsStateMachine of type http://www.w3.org/TR/scxml/ to invoke:

db:section.db:title: Compiling the State Machine
db:section.db:para: We link against the Qt SCXML module by adding the following lines to the example's build files.

db:section.db:title: invoke.pro when using qmake:
db:section.db:para: We then specify the state machine to compile:

db:section.db:title: CMakeLists.txt when using cmake:
db:section.db:programlisting:
find_package(Qt6 REQUIRED COMPONENTS Core Gui Qml Scxml)
target_link_libraries(invoke PRIVATE Qt6::Core Qt6::Gui Qt6::Qml Qt6::Scxml )
db:section.db:para: We then specify the state machine to compile:

The statechart directives STATECHARTS or qt6_add_statecharts invoke the Qt SCXML Compiler, qscxmlc, which is run automatically to generate statemachine.h and statemachine.cpp, which are then added appropriately as headers and sources for compilation.

db:section.db:title: Declaring the state machine as QML element
db:section.db:para: The state machine is declared as a QML element as follows:

db:section.db:title: Instantiating the State Machine
db:section.db:para: We instantiate the generated DirectionsStateMachine element in the MainView.qml file, as follows:
